format_note: Canonical open-operator-asks file (away-ops-rules.md token mechanics). Every entry carries the EXACT reply string(s); the operator replies in the Slack bot channel and the 62.2 handler records it to handoff/operator_tokens.jsonl.

asks.due: 2026-06-15 (HARD: the Agent SDK credit regime starts that day, 3 days into the away window)
asks.ask: From 2026-06-15, headless claude -p sessions draw a separate monthly Agent SDK credit (Max 5x = $100/mo, Max 20x = $200/mo). Two Opus sessions/day for 3 weeks may exceed it. On exhaustion, scheduled sessions STOP until the credit refreshes -- unless usage credits are enabled, which is metered spend (conflicts with the $0 decision). The wrapper already logs per-session cost + LIMIT_HIT lines so the digest shows burn from day 1.
reply_options: SDK CREDIT: ENABLE USAGE CREDITS <monthly cap USD>
asks.recommended: SDK CREDIT: STOP-ON-EXHAUSTION (consistent with the $0 decision; worst case dev pauses until the monthly refresh while trading + digests continue)

asks.ask: ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.pyfinagent.mas-harness.plist is bootout'd but NOT disabled and still on disk -- a reboot + auto-login re-bootstraps its 30-minute loop, racing the away sessions on a different lockfile. Away sessions are hook-blocked from launchctl disable on pyfinagent labels (the 62.0 guard, working as designed), so only you can neutralize it: mv ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.pyfinagent.mas-harness.plist ~/Library/LaunchAgents/disabled.com.pyfinagent.mas-harness.plist.bak
asks.recommended: MAS PLIST: MOVED (run the mv during the 62.7 dress rehearsal)
